project.name: express-fastify-runtime
project.goal: Run existing Express apps on Fastify safely, faster, and without code changes. Production middleware (morgan, helmet, express.json, auth, multer) must work.
project.whatItIs: A hybrid runtime, not a wrapper. Fastify orchestrates; Express runs when required. Compile once at listen(); no dynamic routing.

architecture.summary: Express-Compatible API → compile-time → Route Classifier (safe vs unsafe) → Fastify Lane (compiled) | Embedded Express Lane (real express() instance). Fastify orchestrates; Express runs when required.
architecture.fastifyLane: Request hits Fastify → adapt req/res once → run compiled middleware (preHandler) → run route handler → send.
architecture.expressLane: No Fastify route matched → setNotFoundHandler proxies raw req/res to real Express app → Express runs full stack → response back to Fastify.

guarantees.expressCompatibility: Express apps run unchanged (same API, same middleware order and next()).
guarantees.middlewareSafety: morgan, helmet, express.json(), auth, multer must behave as in Express.
guarantees.performance: Hot paths on Fastify; unsafe paths on real Express. No silent behavior changes; fail fast in dev.

designRules: Routes immutable after listen() — app.use() / app.get() after listen() must throw.
Compile once — No runtime middleware resolution; no dynamic routing.
No guessing safety — If unsure → Express lane. Never silently downgrade.
Express lane = real Express — Use actual express() instance (engine.ts), not a reimplementation.

middlewareClassification.fastifySafe: (req, res, next), no streams, no prototype mutation, no req.app/res.locals. Examples: morgan, helmet, auth, validation.
middlewareClassification.expressRequired: streams (req.pipe), multipart, Express internals. Examples: multer, storage.upload().
middlewareClassification.detection: utils/detect.ts — isExpressRequired(), isFastifySafe(). If unsure → Express lane.
middlewareClassification.expressJson: Intercepted and mapped to Fastify JSON parser. Multipart → Express lane only.

listenAndServer.serverLike: close(callback?), address(). We do not call process.exit; app calls server.close() when it wants to stop.

optimizationPrinciples: Zero alloc hot path — Reusable req/res (createRequestAdapter, createResponseAdapter); mutate per request.
Adapt once per request — preHandler adapts and attaches req/res (kExpressReq, kExpressRes); handler reuses them.
Sync-first handler loop — while loop, index-based next; await only when handler returns thenable.
One Promise when waiting — Only one new Promise + reply.raw.once('finish') when response not yet sent.
Same API, minimal shape — Small req/res surface; no full Express prototype chain.
supported: req.get, header (Referer/Referrer), query, params, body, method, url, headers, path, protocol, secure, ip, ips, hostname, host, xhr, fresh, stale, cookies, signedCookies
req.accepts, acceptsCharsets, acceptsEncodings, acceptsLanguages, req.range, req.is

res.status, send, json, set, setHeader, header, sendStatus, type, contentType, links, jsonp, attachment, append, get, cookie, clearCookie, location (incl. back), redirect, vary, locals, headersSent, end
routerSupport: app.use(path?, router) flattens the router (routes and router.use(path, fn)) when Layer is patched (load express-fastify-runtime before creating routers). Nested routers flattened recursively. RegExp paths → Express lane.
notYet: app.set, app.render, res.sendFile, res.format, req.subdomains, etc. — see docs/EXPRESS_FEATURES.md
expressReference: Express 5.2.1 (express-5.2.1/) is the API reference; see docs/EXPRESS_REFERENCE.md for alignment and mistakes we avoid.

implementationDetails.routeStore: RouteStore holds middleware and route entries until listen(). Then classify → compile → register Fastify routes + mount Express.
implementationDetails.compile: Only fastify-safe routes on Fastify. preHandler runs global middleware and attaches req/res; handler runs route handlers. Unsafe → Express (notFoundHandler proxies).
implementationDetails.expressApp: populateExpressApp() adds all entries to real Express app so Express lane has full stack.
implementationDetails.types: types/express.ts (Express-like); types/internal.ts (RouteEntry, ClassifiedRoute, ServerLike). Align with @types/express-serve-static-core where we support features.

howToContinue: Use Express 5.2.1 (express-5.2.1/) as API reference; see docs/EXPRESS_REFERENCE.md.
Run npm run build, npm test, npm run benchmark.
Pick item from acceptance criteria or not-yet features.
Change only listed modules; keep structure.
Update SPEC.md and EXPRESS_FEATURES.md when adding features.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing Express app runs without modification
File uploads work via multer (Express lane)
Unsafe routes work correctly on Express lane
No silent behavior changes; refactor, don't patch

aiFolderFiles.CONTEXT.md: Full context in markdown for pasting to AI.
aiFolderFiles.context.json: Same context in JSON for tools/scripts.
